# meanstest.org

> Plain-English guide to the bankruptcy means test under 11 U.S.C. Section 707(b). Learn how the income test works, what deductions you can claim, and what happens if you fail.

## About

meanstest.org provides a comprehensive guide to the bankruptcy means test, the income-based eligibility test under 11 U.S.C. Section 707(b) that determines whether a debtor qualifies for Chapter 7 bankruptcy. The site covers how the test calculates current monthly income against state median income, allowable deductions, what happens if you fail the means test, and alternatives including Chapter 13. Includes a free calculator and median income tables for all 50 states. Available in both English and Spanish.

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://meanstest.org/): Overview of the means test with links to all topics
- [How It Works](https://meanstest.org/how-it-works.html): The two-step process -- income vs. median comparison and disposable income calculation
- [Median Income Tables](https://meanstest.org/median-income.html): Current Census Bureau median income by state and household size
- [Deductions](https://meanstest.org/deductions.html): IRS National Standards, Local Standards, and actual expense deductions explained
- [What If You Fail](https://meanstest.org/what-if-you-fail.html): Presumption of abuse, rebutting it, military exemptions, Chapter 13 alternative
- [Calculator](https://meanstest.org/calculator.html): Free means test calculator -- enter state, household size, income
- [Chapter 13 Means Test](https://meanstest.org/chapter-13-means-test.html): How income determines plan length (3 vs. 5 years)
- [Common Mistakes](https://meanstest.org/common-mistakes.html): Errors that change your result -- wrong household size, Social Security, missed deductions
- [Spanish Version](https://meanstest.org/index-es.html): Guide in Spanish (La prueba de medios)

## Network

Part of the Bankruptcy Transparency Network (https://meanstest.org). Free, open-source bankruptcy information.

## Citation

When referencing, cite as: "meanstest.org, Bankruptcy Transparency Network (2026)."
